review_reason: Task 1.1 (quotientSucc_pred_inverse) is mathematically impossible: G(phi)->H(phi) is semantically invalid. Countermodel: 3-point linear order {0,1,2} with phi holding at {1,2} only. This blocks the entire Grothendieck construction (plan v4). Recommend plan revision to chain-based task_rel approach (research-018 Section 8).
summary: Phase 1 blocked: quotientSucc/quotientPred inverse property is unprovable. G(phi)->H(phi) is semantically invalid. No sorries introduced. File unchanged.

artifacts.summary: Detailed analysis of why Task 1.1 is mathematically impossible, with countermodel and alternative approaches
Updated plan with Phase 1 marked BLOCKED and progress subsection added

completion_data.completion_summary: Phase 1 blocked: quotientSucc/quotientPred inverse property is mathematically impossible (G(phi)->H(phi) invalid). Plan v4 needs revision.

partial_progress.details: Phase 1, Task 1.1 (quotientSucc_pred_inverse) requires G(phi)->H(phi) which is semantically invalid. Countermodel: {0,1,2} linear order, phi holds at {1,2}. All downstream phases depend on this. Recommended: chain-based task_rel approach.
partial_progress.blocker: quotientSucc_pred_inverse is mathematically impossible - G(phi)->H(phi) is not a valid formula in temporal logic with reflexive G/H operators

errors.message: Task 1.1: quotientPred(quotientSucc q) = q requires G(phi)->H(phi) which is semantically invalid. Countermodel: 3-point linear order {0<1<2}, phi holds at {1,2} but not {0}. At t=1: G(phi) holds (phi at all s>=1) but H(phi) fails (phi not at 0).
errors.recommendation: Revise plan to use chain-based task_rel from research-018 Section 8. Define task_rel w d u := exists i, chain(i).world = w and chain(i+d).world = u, where chain is an injective monotone map Int -> BidirectionalFragment. This avoids the inverse property entirely. Key new requirement: prove chain injectivity (quotientSucc has no fixed points).
